title: cerastium brachypetalum subsp. corcyrense
intro: Cerastium brachypetalum subsp. corcyrense, also known as the short-petaled mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Cerastium brachypetalum subsp. corcyrense is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
